title: Mammoth Lakes, California Area Attractions
note: Mammoth Lakes is a small mountain resort town. This dataset covers attractions, natural sites, outdoor activities, restaurants, breweries, shops, and day-trip destinations across the Eastern Sierra.

attractions.name: Bar Sierra
attractions.description: Bar Sierra is a contemporary alpine restaurant and bar located within the Outbound Mammoth resort that specializes in California-inspired comfort food. The space features a warm, stylish atmosphere with a massive central fireplace, indoor and outdoor seating, and a casual vibe perfect for groups or families. It also houses an arcade room with free vintage video games, providing a playful nostalgia for guests of all ages
attractions.address: 164 Old Mammoth Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Monday: 4:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Closed Tuesday. (Note: Happy Hour 4:00 PM – 5:30 PM)
attractions.admission: Free entry; food and drink prices vary
highlights: 16 craft beers on tap featuring local Sierra Nevada brews
Free vintage arcade games including Pac-Man
Large indoor central fireplace and outdoor courtyard fire pits
Seasonal happy hour from 4:00 PM to 5:30 PM with food and drink specials
Walk-up window service available for resort pool guests
Private dining room for larger gatherings and events
attractions.tips: Arrive early during happy hour (4:00 PM – 5:30 PM) for the best deals on beer and snacks. The arcade games are completely free, making it a great distraction for kids while waiting for food. If the indoor area is crowded, you can often take your drinks to-go to the outdoor fire pits in the courtyard
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Rock 'N' Bowl, Distant Brewing, Yosemite Axe Throwing, and Mammoth Mountain (approx. 5 minutes away)
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; the restaurant transitions from winter après-ski energy to summer patio dining with fire pits

attractions.name: Base Camp Cafe (now operating as Puerto Vallarta)
attractions.description: Base Camp Cafe is a family-oriented eatery in the heart of Mammoth Lakes, offering a variety of healthy breakfast and lunch options including fresh sandwiches, homemade soups, and vegetarian specialties. Under new ownership, it has transitioned to include Mexican-inspired dishes like fajitas and shrimp soup while maintaining its classic cafe atmosphere. The restaurant is well-known for its quirky 'Outhouse' bathroom decor and friendly, casual service
attractions.address: 3325 Main St, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Daily: 7:30 AM – 4:00 PM (Breakfast and Lunch; some sources mention dinner service until 9:00 PM as Puerto Vallarta)
attractions.admission: Free (Price varies by menu item, with breakfast and lunch dishes typically ranging from $10 to $25)
highlights: Healthy, family-oriented menu with a creative twist on classics
Wide selection of vegetarian and vegan options, including a popular vegan chili
Quirky and famous 'Outhouse' themed bathroom decor
Outdoor seating available for dining in a relaxed mountain setting
All-day breakfast options and fresh, house-made soups
Convenient location on Main Street near major shopping and The Village
attractions.tips: Order the daily specials for significant savings on your meal. Try the 'Kitchen Sink' or 'Southern Cali' omelettes for a hearty post-adventure breakfast. Call ahead for brown bag lunches if you're planning a full day on the trails
attractions.nearby: Schat's Bakery, The Village at Mammoth, Mammoth Mountain Ski Resort, Mammoth Lakes Welcome Center, Convict Lake
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; however, hours and specific menu offerings may shift between the peak summer and winter tourist seasons

attractions.name: Blake DeBock Village at Mammoth Gallery
attractions.description: Blake DeBock Village at Mammoth Gallery is a premier art space in the heart of The Village at Mammoth Lakes, specializing in limited edition fine art photography. The gallery showcases the dramatic landscapes and powerful wildlife of the Sierra Nevada, captured by master photographer Blake DeBock. It offers visitors an immersive fine art experience with prints of extraordinary depth and clarity in epic sizes
attractions.address: 6201 Minaret Road #117, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Winter & Summer: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM Daily; Spring & Fall: Sun 11 AM–6 PM, Mon 12 PM–6 PM, Tue-Wed By Appointment, Thu 12 PM–6 PM, Fri 12 PM–8 PM, Sat 11 AM–8 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Limited edition fine art photography of the Sierra Nevada
Large-format luxury prints suitable for homes and resorts
Photography featuring Mammoth Mountain, Yosemite, and elusive wildlife
Complimentary mockup service to preview art in your space
Located street-level in the vibrant Village at Mammoth
attractions.tips: Take advantage of the complimentary mockup service by bringing a photo of your home wall to see how the art will look. Visit during the evening to enjoy the gallery atmosphere amidst the Village's dining and shopping scene
attractions.nearby: The Village at Mammoth, Mammoth Mountain Gondola, Busy Beez General Store, Alpine Approach
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; hours change seasonally for Spring and Fall

attractions.name: Burgers Restaurant
attractions.description: Burgers Restaurant is a long-standing Mammoth Lakes staple that has been serving the community for over 50 years. Known for its fresh-ground choice beef and hand-formed patties, the restaurant offers a family-friendly atmosphere with a traditional downstairs dining area and a newer upstairs bar featuring craft cocktails and scenic views. Their menu extends beyond burgers to include award-winning pork ribs, steaks, and fresh salads
attractions.address: 6118 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: 11:00 am - until Close
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Choice beef ground fresh daily and hand-formed patties
Award-winning pork baby back ribs
Upstairs bar with craft cocktails and mountain views
Classic "Mud Pie" dessert
Family-oriented with a dedicated children's menu
attractions.tips: The restaurant is highly popular; arrive before 5:00 PM to avoid long wait times. Be sure to try the 'Mud Pie' for dessert, which is a local favorite. Parking in their private lot is limited, but they sometimes offer a credit if you have to pay for parking nearby
attractions.nearby: The Village at Mammoth, Village Gondola, Alpenhof Lodge, Clocktower Cellar
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; popular with both summer hikers and winter skiers

attractions.name: Campo Mammoth Village
attractions.description: Campo Mammoth is a rustic Italian restaurant located in the heart of The Village at Mammoth, specializing in wood-fired Neapolitan pizzas and farm-to-table cuisine. Developed by James Beard-nominated chef Mark Estee, the eatery offers a casual yet lively atmosphere perfect for après-ski dining or family gatherings. Its menu features handcrafted salumi, fresh pasta, and an extensive selection of wine and cocktails
attractions.address: 6201 Minaret Rd, Suite 240, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Happy Hour: 4:00 PM – 5:30 PM; Dinner: 5:00 PM – 9:30 PM
attractions.admission: Price varies
highlights: Wood-fired Neapolitan pizzas cooked in a traditional brick oven
Handcrafted salumi and farm-fresh seasonal produce
Expansive outdoor patio with fire pits and heaters for year-round dining
Award-winning rustic Italian menu designed by Chef Mark Estee
Extensive list of curated wines and signature cocktails
Popular après-ski destination with daily happy hour specials
attractions.tips: Try the wood-fired pizzas, particularly the 'Bee Sting' or 'Michelangelo'. Reservations are highly recommended during peak ski weekends and holidays as the restaurant is a top village choice. For the best ambiance, request a table on the outdoor patio near the fire pits to enjoy the mountain stars
attractions.nearby: The Village Lodge, Village Gondola, Gomez's Mexican Restaurant, Mammoth Brewing Company, Mammoth Ice Rink
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; hours may adjust during shoulder seasons

attractions.name: Convict Lake
attractions.description: Convict Lake is a stunning alpine lake in the Sherwin Range of the Sierra Nevada, celebrated for its turquoise-blue water and dramatic mountain backdrop, including the 12,241-foot Mount Morrison. The area offers world-class trout fishing, scenic hiking, and boat rentals, and it is famous for its history involving an 1871 shootout with escaped prisoners
attractions.address: 2000 Convict Lake Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Lake: Open 24 hours daily. General Store: Mon–Sat 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Sun 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Restaurant: Lounge opens at 5:00 PM, Dining Room at 5:30 PM. Campground/Marina: Open late April through October (seasonal)
attractions.admission: Free for general lake access and hiking; Day use parking is approximately $5. Boat rental prices vary: $180–$340 for 2-hour minimums and $410–$800 for full-day rentals depending on craft type
highlights: 3-mile loop trail around the lake shoreline
Exceptional trout fishing for Rainbow and Brown trout
Stunning alpine scenery featuring Mount Morrison and Laurel Mountain
Marina with pontoon, motorboat, and kayak rentals
Historical site of the 1871 convict shootout
Horseback riding excursions available through the resort pack station
attractions.tips: Arrive early to secure parking as the lot fills quickly during peak summer months. For the loop hike, starting clockwise provides better views and earlier sun exposure. Restaurant reservations are strongly recommended as it is a popular destination for locals and tourists alike
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Yosemite Airport, John Muir Wilderness, Crowley Lake, and the town of Mammoth Lakes
attractions.seasonal_notes: The resort and restaurant are open year-round; however, the campground and marina are seasonal, operating from late April (general fishing season opener) until the end of October. High-elevation trails may be snow-covered until early summer

attractions.name: Crowley Lake
attractions.description: Crowley Lake is a massive 12-mile-long reservoir in the Eastern Sierra, created in 1941 by the Long Valley Dam. It is renowned as a premier trout fishery and a hub for water sports like kayaking, water skiing, and wakeboarding. The lake is also home to the famous Crowley Lake Columns, unique volcanic geological formations along its eastern shore
attractions.address: 1149 S Landing Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Front Gate: Sun–Thu 6am–9pm, Fri–Sat 6am–10pm; Tackle Shop: Daily 6am–6pm
attractions.admission: Daily Parking: $10; Lake Use: $10; Dock Slip: $15–$30/night; RV Sites: $60/night; Dry Camping: $25/night
highlights: World-class trout fishing
Crowley Lake Columns
Full-service marina with boat rentals and over 200 slips
Pelican Point Grill
Water sports including wakeboarding, water skiing, and jet skiing
attractions.tips: The Crowley Lake Columns are best reached by boat when the road is closed for repairs, or via a 4WD high-clearance vehicle for shore access. Restricted fishing (artificial lures only) begins August 1st
attractions.nearby: McGee Creek, Convict Lake, Rock Creek Canyon, and Mammoth Lakes Basin
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open for the season from the last Saturday in April (Opener) through October 31 (Boating) or November 15 (Shore Fishing); gates close daily for Quagga mussel protection

attractions.name: Crystal Lake Trail
attractions.description: Crystal Lake Trail is a steep but rewarding 3-mile out-and-back hike that leads to a stunning alpine lake tucked beneath the granite monolith of Crystal Crag. The trail offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Mammoth Lakes Basin, including Lake George and Lake Mary, as it ascends through shaded pine forests. It is a popular choice for families and hikers looking for a short adventure with a high-payoff destination
attractions.address: Lake George Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours; road access to the trailhead is seasonal, typically from late May to mid-October
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Panoramic views of Lake Mary, Lake George, and the Lakes Basin
Stunning backdrop of the 10,377-foot Crystal Crag monolith
Pristine alpine waters of Crystal Lake for fishing and swimming
Series of well-maintained switchbacks through pine and fir forests
Opportunities for backcountry camping and viewing wildflowers
attractions.tips: Arrive early as parking at the Lake George trailhead fills up quickly; consider taking the free Mammoth Lakes Basin shuttle/tram; bring plenty of water and wear sturdy hiking boots for the rocky switchbacks
attractions.nearby: Lake George, Crystal Crag, Lake Mary, Lake George Campground, Mammoth Crest Trail
attractions.seasonal_notes: Accessible primarily in summer and fall; road access to Lake George closes during winter months due to snow

attractions.name: Devils Postpile National Monument
attractions.description: Established in 1911, Devils Postpile National Monument protects a rare geologic wonder featuring 60-foot high basalt columns formed by ancient lava flows. The monument also encompasses the stunning 101-foot Rainbow Falls and serves as a gateway to the High Sierra wilderness. It offers visitors a chance to explore unique geologic formations, lush meadows, and pristine river ecosystems
attractions.address: Devils Postpile Access Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Monument and valley are normally open 24 hours daily during the season. Ranger Station (2026): Thu-Sun 9:00 am - 3:00 pm (July 2 – Sept 20); Sat-Sun 9:00 am - 3:00 pm (Sept 21 – Nov 1)
attractions.days_open: Daily during the operating season (typically mid-June to mid-October). In 2026, road access is restricted due to construction: open Thursday to Sunday (July 6 – Sept 20) and Saturday to Sunday (Sept 21 – Nov 1)
attractions.admission: Free entrance to the monument. However, a mandatory shuttle bus fee applies: $15 for adults, $7 for children (ages 3-15), and free for children 2 and under. For exception vehicles allowed to drive in, a $10 standard amenity fee applies
highlights: Devils Postpile basalt columns
Rainbow Falls
Middle Fork of the San Joaquin River
Soda Springs Meadow
Ansel Adams and John Muir Wilderness access
Minaret Falls
attractions.tips: Use the mandatory shuttle bus from Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center during the day to avoid parking issues. Bring plenty of water and be prepared for variable mountain weather. Leashed and muzzled dogs are allowed on the shuttle. Vehicles over 25 feet are restricted on the spur road
attractions.nearby: Reds Meadow Resort, Minaret Vista, Mammoth Mountain, Ansel Adams Wilderness, and John Muir Wilderness
attractions.seasonal_notes: Seasonal (typically mid-June to mid-October). Closed in winter due to snow. In 2026, significant road construction limits access to specific days of the week

attractions.name: Distant Brewing
attractions.description: Distant Brewing is an award-winning micro-brewery and restaurant in Mammoth Lakes, California, known for its artfully crafted beers and community-focused atmosphere. Originally established as Black Doubt Brewing in 2015, it rebranded and moved to its current location in 2019 to provide a larger space for visitors. The venue features a 22-seat bar, a stage for live music, pool tables, and a seasonal outdoor beer garden
attractions.address: 568 Old Mammoth Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Monday–Thursday: 12:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Friday–Saturday: 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 9:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: 22-seat bar, comfortable lounge seating, and a seasonal outdoor beer garden
Award-winning craft beers, including the 'On the Radar' Pale Ale
Live music on Thursday and Sunday nights and Open Mic Mondays
Weekly 8-ball pool tournament hosted by Eastern Sierra Pool on Wednesdays
Full food menu featuring smashburgers, pizzas, sandwiches, and appetizers like house-made beer cheese
Dog and kid-friendly environment with free WiFi and off-street parking
attractions.tips: Happy hour runs mid-week from 3:00 PM to 5:00 PM, offering discounts on all drinks including house beers and non-alcoholic options. Seating is first-come, first-served as the brewery does not take reservations. Be sure to check out the 'Sky Pillow' Hazy IPA or the 'Black Doubt' Imperial Stout, which are local favorites
attractions.nearby: Sierra Center Mall, Vons, Mammoth Mountain, and various local shops on Old Mammoth Road
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; the outdoor beer garden is open seasonally

attractions.name: Duck Pass Trail
attractions.description: The Duck Pass Trail climbs through the Coldwater Creek watershed to its origins at Duck Pass, following a series of spectacular alpine lakes and meadows within the John Muir Wilderness. The trail passes Barney Lake, Blue Crag, and the Mammoth Crest before reaching the pass and descending to the deep-blue Duck Lake. It is widely considered one of the most scenic and rewarding high-altitude hikes in the Eastern Sierra
attractions.address: Coldwater Creek Trailhead (at the southeastern end of Coldwater Campground), Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546 (GPS: 37.5912°N, -118.9892°W)
attractions.hours: Trailhead: 24/7; Ranger Station: 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM (hours vary seasonally)
attractions.admission: Free for day use; Wilderness permit required for overnight trips
highlights: Passes several stunning alpine lakes including Arrowhead, Skelton, and Barney
Panoramic views from the 10,800-foot summit of Duck Pass
Access to the expansive John Muir Wilderness and the deep-blue Duck Lake
Excellent opportunities for high-altitude fishing and wildflower viewing
Option to continue to Pika Lake for additional scenery
attractions.tips: Acclimatize to the 9,000+ foot altitude before starting, bring at least 2 liters of water, and start early to avoid afternoon thunderstorms. Wear sturdy hiking boots as the climb up Duck Pass involves loose rock
attractions.nearby: Coldwater Campground, Lake Mary, Emerald Lake, Horseshoe Lake, and the Mammoth Lakes Basin
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open summer and fall; winter access is limited to skis or snowshoes; trailhead may close in late fall for forest restoration

attractions.name: Earthquake Fault
attractions.description: The Earthquake Fault is a dramatic geological fissure that cuts up to 60 feet deep and 10 feet wide through glassy rhyolite lava flows. Formed by tectonic stresses between 200 and 600 years ago, it is technically a fissure rather than a fault as there has been little vertical or lateral movement between the rock walls. Visitors can explore the feature via a short, 0.3-mile interpretive walking trail that winds through a scenic forest of old-growth Red Fir and Jeffrey Pine
attractions.address: Minaret Road (Hwy 203), Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (); however, road access and visibility are seasonally restricted by snow ()
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Dramatic 60-foot deep and 10-foot wide volcanic fissure
0.3-mile easy, self-guided interpretive walking trail
Cool air and year-round snow often found at the bottom of the chasm
Scenic picnic area shaded by old-growth Jeffrey pine and Red Fir trees
Unique geological history linked to the Inyo Domes and volcanic dike system
attractions.tips: Watch your footing on the uneven stone steps and stay behind the safety fences. Bring a light jacket even in summer, as the temperature drops significantly near the fissure ()
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Mountain Ski Resort, Minaret Vista, Inyo Craters, Mammoth Lakes Welcome Center
attractions.seasonal_notes: Best visited from mid-summer through late fall when the ground is clear of snow. During winter and spring, the fissure may be obscured by snow, and the access road may be impassable ()

attractions.name: Footloose Sports
attractions.description: Footloose Sports is a premier outdoor gear and ski shop in Mammoth Lakes, outfitting adventures for over 40 years. They offer top-quality gear, expert advice, and specialized services like custom boot fitting for skiing, mountain biking, and hiking in the Eastern Sierra
attractions.address: 3043 Main Street, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Sunday – Thursday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Friday & Saturday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free entry; rental and service prices vary (e.g., Adult Standard Ski Package from $53/day, Full Suspension Bike from $100/day, Paddleboard from $80/day)
highlights: Expert custom ski boot fitting and orthotics
Premium ski and snowboard rentals and demos
Mountain bike and e-bike rentals for all levels
Inflatable stand-up paddleboard (SUP) rentals
Extensive selection of outdoor apparel, footwear, and accessories
Full-service ski and bike repair shop
attractions.tips: Rent equipment after 3 PM for the next day to save time, and book seasonal rentals early starting in October. You can also apply up to two days of rental credit towards the purchase of a new bike or paddleboard
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Mountain Ski Resort, The Village at Mammoth, Devils Postpile National Monument, Rainbow Falls, and various alpine lakes like Lake Mary
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; features ski/snowboard services in winter and bike/paddleboard services in summer

attractions.name: Giovanni's Restaurant and Bar
attractions.description: Giovanni's has been a staple in Mammoth Lakes since 1986, serving locals and visitors fresh pizza, handmade Italian entrees, and a variety of salads. The restaurant features a family-friendly dining room and a sports bar with high-definition TVs for catching the game. Known for its signature "Mammoth" sized pizzas, it offers a casual atmosphere perfect for refueling after a day in the Eastern Sierra
attractions.address: 437 Old Mammoth Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546-2013
attractions.hours: Daily: Lunch 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Dinner 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM (Hours may vary seasonally; closed on Thanksgiving and Christmas)
attractions.admission: Free (Price of food varies; Pizzas range from $11.90 to $33.90+)
highlights: Signature "Mammoth" 19-inch pizzas
Full sports bar with HD televisions
Extensive menu including pasta, wings, and fresh salads
Vegetarian, vegan cheese, and gluten-free crust options
Family-friendly environment with a dedicated kids' menu
Located conveniently in the Minaret Village Shopping Center
attractions.tips: Arrive early (around 5:30 PM) to beat the dinner rush, as the restaurant does not accept reservations. If you're looking for a quieter experience, the dining area is typically less rowdy than the sports bar. Try the "Take and Bake" pizza option if you want to enjoy their famous pizza at your lodge
attractions.nearby: Minaret Village Shopping Center, Vons, Mammoth Rock 'n' Bowl, Mammoth Creek Park
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; closed on Thanksgiving and Christmas Day

attractions.name: Gomez's Mexican Restaurant & Cantina
attractions.description: Gomez's Mexican Restaurant & Cantina is a family-friendly establishment in the Village at Mammoth, known for its extensive collection of tequilas and the "world-famous" Mammoth Margarita. The menu features traditional Mexican and Latin fusion cuisine prepared with high-quality ingredients, including hormone-free meats and fresh salsas. It offers a lively atmosphere with both indoor dining and a summer patio featuring a sandbox and horseshoe pit
attractions.address: 100 Canyon Blvd, Suite 225, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Noon - 8:30 PM Daily
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Extensive tequila collection with nearly 1,000 bottles
Home of the signature "Mammoth Margarita"
Conveniently located in The Village at Mammoth near the gondola
Family-friendly atmosphere with a children's sandbox and kids menu
High-quality ingredients including hormone-free beef, chicken, and pork
Summer outdoor dining with a regulation horseshoe pit
attractions.tips: Reservations are not accepted, so arrive early during peak times. Take-out is available but subject to kitchen capacity. Note that an 18% gratuity is added for parties of 6 or more, and a 12% service charge applies to to-go orders
attractions.nearby: The Village at Mammoth, Village Gondola, Burgers Restaurant, Skadi, Toomey's
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; outdoor dining and horseshoe pit available in summer months

attractions.name: Good Life Cafe
attractions.description: Good Life Cafe is a long-standing, locally-owned eatery in Mammoth Lakes that has been serving the community since 1994 (). The cafe specializes in healthy, fresh American and Mexican-inspired cuisine, offering a variety of breakfast, lunch, and dinner options (). It is highly regarded for its inclusive menu, which features numerous vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free choices in a casual atmosphere ()
attractions.address: 126 Old Mammoth Road, The Mammoth Mall, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Sunday: 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Monday: 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Thursday: 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Saturday: 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM
attractions.admission: Price varies; typical entrees range from $13 to $18
highlights: Locally owned and operated since 1994
Extensive menu options for vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free diets
Outdoor deck seating available for summer dining
Famous for breakfast dishes served daily until 3:00 PM
Convenient location within the Mammoth Mall with easy parking
attractions.tips: Note the early 3:00 PM closing times on Tuesdays and Wednesdays (). Standout menu items include the Arturo's Rancheros and zucchini fries (). It is often less crowded than Village breakfast spots, making it a great choice for faster service ()
attractions.nearby: Yosemite Axe Throwing, Outbound Mammoth, Mono Arts Council, and Mammoth Minaret Cinemas
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; outdoor seating is available during the summer months

attractions.name: Horseshoe Lake
attractions.description: Horseshoe Lake is a scenic alpine lake at the end of Lake Mary Road, uniquely known as the only lake in the Mammoth Lakes Basin where swimming is permitted. The area is famous for its approximately 120 acres of 'tree kill' caused by naturally occurring carbon dioxide (CO2) gas seeping from cooling magma below the surface. It serves as a popular destination for hiking, mountain biking, paddleboarding, and as a trailhead for backcountry adventures
attractions.address: 4500 Lake Mary Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Day use only; Open 24 hours for trail access
attractions.admission: Free ()
highlights: Only lake in the Mammoth Lakes Basin that allows swimming
Fascinating 120-acre CO2 tree-kill area with educational signage
Horseshoe Lake Loop: An easy 1.8-mile trail for hiking and beginner mountain biking
Staging point for Mammoth Pass, McLeod Lake, and John Muir Wilderness trails
Large sandy beaches during dry years and summer months
Stunning views of the Mammoth Crest and southern flank of Mammoth Mountain
attractions.tips: Be aware of CO2 danger signs; avoid sitting in low-lying depressions or enclosures where gas can collect. Bring a hammock for relaxing on the sandy beaches. The lake level can drop significantly in dry years
attractions.nearby: McLeod Lake, Lake Mary, Twin Falls Overlook, Mammoth Mountain, Devils Postpile National Monument
attractions.seasonal_notes: Best visited late May through early October; road access is typically closed in winter due to heavy snow, though accessible via ski or snowshoe

attractions.name: Hot Creek Geologic Site
attractions.description: Hot Creek Geologic Site is a stunning destination where visitors can witness geology in action through boiling turquoise pools, fumaroles, and periodic geyser eruptions. Located within the Long Valley Caldera, the site features steaming water heated by a magma chamber just three miles below the surface. While the area is breathtakingly beautiful, the water temperatures are erratic and dangerous, making entering the water strictly prohibited
attractions.address: Hot Creek Hatchery Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Boiling turquoise hot springs and turquoise pools
Active fumaroles and steam vents
Dramatic Sierra Nevada mountain backdrops
Geological interpretive trails and overlooks
World-class fly fishing nearby in the cooler sections of the creek
Photography opportunities, particularly at Brees Lookout
attractions.tips: Swimming or entering the water is strictly prohibited due to extreme and unpredictable temperatures; stay behind safety fences at all times. There is no potable water or cell service available at the site, so bring your own supplies. Dogs are allowed but must remain on a leash
attractions.nearby: Convict Lake, Hot Creek Fish Hatchery, Mammoth Yosemite Airport, Devils Postpile National Monument, Crowley Lake
attractions.seasonal_notes: The site is accessible year-round, but the access road is unpaved and typically closed to vehicles during winter. In winter months, visitors must access the site via snowmobile, snowshoes, or cross-country skis

attractions.name: Inyo Craters
attractions.description: The Inyo Craters are a series of three volcanic explosion pits formed by violent steam blasts approximately 600 years ago. Two of the craters contain unique emerald green lakes, while the third sits atop Deer Mountain and is typically dry. The site is a popular destination for hiking, offering a short and accessible trail through an old-growth Jeffrey pine forest
attractions.address: Mammoth Scenic Loop, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: 6:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. (Day use only)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Three north-south-aligned phreatic explosion craters
Two craters featuring small, vibrant emerald green lakes
A 1.4-mile round-trip uphill hiking trail through old-growth Jeffrey pine forest
Interpretive information at the trailhead explaining the volcanic history
Part of the larger Mono-Inyo Craters volcanic chain
Panoramic views of the San Joaquin Ridge and White Mountains from the rim
attractions.tips: The 1.3-mile unpaved road to the trailhead is rough but usually passable for 2WD vehicles in summer; use caution after rain or snow. Dogs are allowed but must be leashed in developed recreation sites. Be sure to stay behind the gated overlooks at the crater rims for safety
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Mountain, Earthquake Fault, Mono Craters, Obsidian Dome, June Lake Loop
attractions.seasonal_notes: Accessible year-round; however, the unpaved access road is typically only passable for vehicles from June through mid-October due to heavy snow. Winter access is via snowshoes, skis, or snowmobile

attractions.name: John's Pizza Works
attractions.description: Established in 1987, John's Pizza Works is a staple in Mammoth Lakes known for its fresh, hand-tossed pizzas and house-made sauces. The venue is uniquely divided into a family-friendly dining area with a video arcade and ice cream parlor, alongside The Outlaw Saloon, a popular sports bar featuring numerous HD TVs. It offers a diverse menu that includes wings, pasta, and hot sandwiches, making it a versatile destination for both families and sports fans
attractions.address: 3499 Main Street, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 11:30 am - 10:30 pm; Sunday: 10:00 am - 10:30 pm (Early open for NFL Football Sundays)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Fresh pizza dough made from scratch daily
In-house crafted pasta and wing sauces including the 'Matsu' Asian-inspired flavor
The Outlaw Saloon, a Western-modern sports bar with 80-inch HD flat-screen TVs
Family-oriented entertainment including a video arcade and Two Scoops Ice Cream Parlor
Extensive late-night menu available until closing
Large selection of premium spirits, cold beer, and select wines

attractions.name: Kittredge Sports
attractions.description: For over 60 years, Kittredge Sports has served as a premier 4-season outdoor retailer in the Eastern Sierra. The shop offers an extensive selection of high-quality apparel and equipment, specializing in ski rentals, expert boot fitting, and professional fly fishing guides. Their knowledgeable staff is dedicated to helping visitors find the right gear for mountain adventures in any season

Leading-edge ski tuning and overnight repair shop

attractions.name: Lake George
attractions.description: Lake George is a stunning 38-acre alpine lake nestled in the Mammoth Lakes Basin at an elevation of over 9,000 feet. It holds the distinction of being the deepest lake in the basin, reaching depths of over 200 feet, and is the highest lake in the area accessible by vehicle. The lake is framed by the dramatic granite spire of Crystal Crag, providing one of the most scenic backdrops in the Eastern Sierra
attractions.address: Lake George Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Lake area: Generally accessible 24/7 during the summer season; Lake George Dockside Shop: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Summer only); Campground quiet hours: 10:00 PM - 6:00 AM
attractions.days_open: Daily during the operating season (typically late May through early October)
attractions.admission: Free for day use and hiking; Campground sites are $34 per night; Small fee may apply for boat launching
highlights: Deepest lake in the Mammoth Lakes Basin at over 200 feet deep
Highest drive-to lake in the Mammoth Lakes area, situated at 9,000 feet
Spectacular views of the iconic Crystal Crag granite monolith.jpg)
Major trailhead for hikes to Crystal Lake, TJ Lake, and Barrett Lake
Renowned for excellent trout fishing, particularly for large rainbow trout
Peaceful environment with no motorboats or swimming permitted on the lake
attractions.tips: Arrive early (before 8:00 AM) to secure parking, as the lot is shared between campers and hikers and fills quickly on weekends. As this is an active bear area, always use the provided bear-proof storage containers for food and scented items. Tucked between Lake Mary and Twin Lakes, it is surrounded by lush forest and tall grasses, offering stunning views of the Mammoth Crest and Crystal Crag. It is a popular spot for trout fishing, kayaking, and photography, particularly at the nearby Twin Falls overlook

Twin Falls overlook where the lake drains into Twin Lakes
Wildyrie Lodge boat rentals and historic cabins
Picturesque picnic areas just above the Twin Lakes drainage
Secluded and quiet atmosphere compared to larger neighboring lakes

It is a premier destination for trout fishing, boating, and lakeside camping, offering breathtaking alpine scenery surrounded by lodgepole pines and rugged Sierra peaks

They roast their own gourmet coffee beans and prepare fresh pastries, muffins, and breakfast burritos in-house daily. The shop offers a cozy atmosphere with indoor seating and an outdoor dog-friendly patio

attractions.description: Main Street Promenade offers a unique mountain shopping experience in the heart of Mammoth Lakes, CA. This one-stop destination features a diverse blend of shops, including pubs, art galleries, a grand Sun & Ski shop for year-round sports gear, and local essentials like a laundromat and market. Visitors can enjoy hand-brewed coffee, live concerts, and a relaxed atmosphere while exploring the promenade

attractions.name: Mammoth Adventure Center
attractions.description: Located at the base of Mammoth Mountain's Main Lodge, the Mammoth Adventure Center is the premier hub for alpine family fun in the Eastern Sierra. It serves as the gateway to the world-class Mammoth Bike Park, scenic gondola rides to the 11,053-ft summit, and a variety of high-altitude thrills. Visitors can enjoy activities ranging from guided climbing on the Via Ferrata to bungee trampolines and climbing walls
attractions.address: 10001 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546

attractions.nearby: Devils Postpile National Monument, Rainbow Falls, Minaret Vista, and the Lakes Basin

attractions.name: Mammoth Lakes Basin
attractions.description: The Mammoth Lakes Basin is a spectacular glacial cirque featuring more than a dozen alpine lakes, including Lake Mary, Lake Mamie, Lake George, and Horseshoe Lake. It is a premier destination for outdoor recreation, offering extensive opportunities for hiking, fishing, boating, and camping against a backdrop of jagged granite peaks like Crystal Crag. Located just minutes from the town of Mammoth Lakes, it serves as a high-alpine gateway to the John Muir Wilderness (,)
attractions.address: Lake Mary Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Day use only; certain facilities like marinas and stores typically operate 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM during the summer season
attractions.admission: Free; fees apply for camping, boat rentals, and wilderness permits

Multiple marinas offering boat, kayak, and paddleboard rentals during the summer season
Scenic natural features like the cascading Twin Falls and the dramatic granite buttress of Crystal Crag
Excellent fishing for stocked trout across several accessible lakes
attractions.tips: Utilize the free Lakes Basin Trolley to avoid parking congestion at trailheads and marinas. Arrive early as parking lots often fill by mid-morning during peak summer months. attractions.name: Mammoth Lakes Town Loop
attractions.description: The Town Loop is a 7.8-mile paved multi-use path that encircles the scenic community of Mammoth Lakes, providing access to neighborhoods, businesses, and recreational amenities. It features sweeping views of the Sherwin Range and Mammoth Mountain, winding through pine forests and along Mammoth Creek. The trail is mostly off-road, making it ideal for families, beginner cyclists, and those looking to adjust to the high altitude

Sweeping vistas of the Sherwin Range and White Mountains

Multi-use functionality for biking, hiking, and e-biking

attractions.name: Mammoth Lakes Trail System
attractions.description: The Mammoth Lakes Trail System offers boundless recreation opportunities on more than 300 miles of trails, ranging from granite crags to trout-stocked lakes. Managed jointly by the Town of Mammoth Lakes and the Inyo National Forest, the system provides connections to the Pacific Crest Trail, Devils Postpile National Monument, and three wilderness areas. It is designed for all seasons and abilities, accommodating both motorized and non-motorized activities

Access to iconic destinations like the Lakes Basin and Inyo Craters
The 7.8-mile paved Town Loop for family-friendly recreation
Joint management by the Town of Mammoth Lakes and Inyo National Forest

The center also features interpretive displays and a well-stocked gift shop with educational books and souvenirs

Host to the seasonal Mammoth Lakes Farmer’s Market
Convenient central location with diverse retail options including souvenir shops

At the top, visitors can explore the Eleven53 Interpretive Center, enjoy lunch with a view at the Eleven53 Café, and take photos at the famous summit totem sign. The experience is suitable for all ages and is accessible to non-skiers

highlights: Aerial ascent to 11,053 feet elevation
Eleven53 Interpretive Center with hands-on Sierra Nevada exhibits

attractions.name: Mammoth Mountain Ski Area

The resort is renowned for its long seasons, often extending from November into June or even July

attractions.name: Mammoth Rock Trail

It passes directly beneath the iconic Mammoth Rock, a 250-million-year-old limestone and marble monolith, offering panoramic views of the town and valley. The trail is shared by hikers, mountain bikers, and horseback riders, making it a versatile local favorite

Situated at an elevation of 9,265 feet, it provides an unobstructed vantage point for witnessing vibrant mountain sunsets and exceptional stargazing. The site features interpretive storyboards that identify the prominent peaks and geological features of the surrounding Ansel Adams Wilderness

attractions.name: Mono Arts Council
attractions.category: Museum / Art Gallery / Community Art Center
attractions.description: The Mono Arts Council (MAC) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to fostering a lasting culture of creativity in Mono County through education, advocacy, and community programs. Its Community Arts Center & Gallery in Mammoth Lakes showcases the work of over 70 local and regional artists, providing a space for both art appreciation and hands-on creation (,)
attractions.address: 501 Old Mammoth Road, #5, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546, USA
attractions.hours: Friday - Monday: 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM; Closed Tuesday - Thursday
attractions.admission: Free for Gallery; Class and workshop prices vary (typically $75–$145); Summer camps approximately $150–$325
highlights: Retail art gallery featuring works from over 70 local and regional artists
K-12 arts education programs including visual arts and music in local schools
Monthly 'Art & Wine' classes and various creative workshops for adults
Community 'Open Studio' hours allowing visitors to drop in and create
Seasonal summer art camps for children aged 4-12
Host of major annual events like the Labor Day Arts Festival and July 4th Art Show
attractions.tips: Check the online calendar for the current 'Art & Wine' class schedule and seasonal exhibits. The gallery is an excellent spot to find unique, locally-made gifts. Community Open Studio hours (12pm-5pm during open days) offer a great way to engage in self-guided creative projects (,)
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Lakes Welcome Center, Edison Theatre, Mammoth Museum at the Hayden Cabin, Minaret Village Shopping Center (Vons), Giovanni's Restaurant
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; hosts major outdoor art festivals during the summer months (July 4th and Labor Day weekend)

attractions.name: Nik-N-Willie's Pizza & Subs
attractions.description: Nik-N-Willie's is a local favorite in Mammoth Lakes known for serving a wide variety of tasty traditional and unique pizzas. The menu also features hot and cold sandwiches, fresh salads, and soups, complemented by a selection of twenty beers and various wines. It is a popular spot for both dine-in and takeout, including their signature take-and-bake options
attractions.address: 100 Old Mammoth Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 11:30 AM - 09:00 PM
attractions.admission: Price varies
highlights: Specialty pizzas including the "Nik and Wild Willie" and the spicy "Thai Pie"
Extensive selection of hot and cold subs such as the "Hot Yardbird" and "Hot Pastrami"
Take-n-Bake pizza options for those who prefer to bake at home
Beer and wine selection featuring 20 different beers
Weekday lunch specials and pizza by the slice
Vegetarian-friendly menu options
attractions.tips: Parking can be extremely difficult as there are only a few dedicated spaces available. The "Thai Pie" is highly recommended for those who enjoy a spicy kick, and the "Take-n-Bake" option is ideal for travelers staying in local condos
attractions.nearby: Tesla Supercharger station, Mammoth Creek Park, and various shops on Old Mammoth Road
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round

attractions.name: Obsidian Dome Observation Site
attractions.description: Obsidian Dome is a massive volcanic feature composed primarily of black obsidian glass, formed during an eruption around 1350 CE. Part of the Mono-Inyo Craters chain, it offers a dramatic, moon-like landscape with boulders of glass and panoramic views of the surrounding Long Valley Caldera. It is a popular spot for geology enthusiasts, hikers, and those looking to explore unique volcanic terrain
attractions.address: Glass Flow Rd / Obsidian Dome Rd (Off Hwy 395), Mono County, CA 93546. GPS: 37.757829, -119.026431
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours/day
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Massive obsidian boulders, some the size of cars
Short hiking trail (approx. 0.5-1 mile) to the summit for panoramic vistas
Unique, moon-like volcanic landscape with sharp glass formations
Historical site for Native American obsidian trading and tool-making
Access to nearby Glass Creek for a stark contrast in scenery
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy, closed-toe shoes as the obsidian rocks are razor-sharp. There is no shade on the dome, so bring water, sunscreen, and a hat. While the dirt road is often passable for 2WD cars, a high-clearance vehicle is recommended for the rougher sections
attractions.nearby: Inyo Craters, June Lake Loop, Glass Creek, Minaret Vista, June Mountain
attractions.seasonal_notes: Accessible year-round; however, vehicle access is typically summer/fall only due to snow. In winter, the site is popular for cross-country skiing and snowshoeing via groomed trails

attractions.name: Panorama Gondola at Mammoth Mountain
attractions.description: The Panorama Gondola is a scenic aerial ride at Mammoth Mountain that transports visitors to the summit at 11,053 feet. At the top, guests can enjoy 360-degree views of the Sierra Nevada, explore the Eleven53 Interpretive Center, and dine at the highest café in California. It is a family-friendly attraction accessible to both skiers and non-skiers
attractions.address: 10001 Minaret Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Daily 8:30 AM – 3:30 PM (Last ride down at 3:30 PM)
attractions.admission: Adults: $23–$40; Kids (12 & under): Free (up to 2 per paying adult); Seniors (80+): Free; Ikon Pass holders: Free. Prices vary by date and booking method
highlights: 360-degree panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada and Minarets from 11,053 feet elevation
Eleven53 Interpretive Center featuring hands-on exhibits on local geology and natural history
Eleven53 Café, the highest eatery in California, located at the summit
Famous summit totem sign, a popular spot for photos and selfies
Access to several high-alpine hiking trails for one-way or round-trip excursions
attractions.tips: Book online in advance to save up to 20% on ticket prices. Bring a telephoto lens for mountain photography and dress in layers, as the summit is often much windier and colder than the base. During summer, leashed dogs are allowed on the gondola
attractions.nearby: Main Lodge Adventure Center, Woolly’s Adventure Summit (Mountain Coaster & Tube Park), Minaret Vista, and various hiking/mountain biking trails
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round, weather permitting, with seasonal maintenance closures (typically late May to mid-June and late September to mid-November)

attractions.name: Petra's Bistro & Wine Bar
attractions.description: Petra’s Bistro & Wine Bar was a premier dining destination in Mammoth Lakes, offering a blend of Contemporary American cuisine and a sophisticated yet warm atmosphere. Located within the historic Alpenhof Lodge, it was celebrated for its extensive wine selection and daily changing seasonal menus. The restaurant served the community for 21 years before closing its doors in early 2024
attractions.address: 6080 Minaret Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Historically 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Happy Hour was typically 5:00 PM – 6:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free; menu prices varied
highlights: Contemporary American cuisine with a menu that changed daily to reflect seasonal ingredients
Extensive wine list featuring over 30 wines by the glass via Mammoth's only Cruvinet system
Sophisticated, warm atmosphere with a fireplace and an Austrian-inspired lodge setting
Handcrafted cocktails and a wide selection of local craft beers
Spacious dog-friendly outdoor patio for summer dining
Named after the owners' mother, Petra, and run as a family business for over two decades
attractions.tips: While it was open, reservations were highly recommended at least a week in advance for weekend dining; the bar area was a great spot for walk-ins and enjoying the Cruvinet wine selection
attractions.nearby: The Village at Mammoth, Clocktower Cellar, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, Mammoth Brewing Company
attractions.seasonal_notes: Permanently closed as of April 2024; historically open year-round with seasonal menu adjustments

attractions.name: Rainbow Falls
attractions.description: Rainbow Falls is the highest waterfall on the Middle Fork of the San Joaquin River, plunging 101 feet into the canyon below. It is aptly named for the colorful rainbows that frequently appear in its mist on sunny summer days. The site is a popular destination for hikers and is managed as part of the Devils Postpile National Monument
attractions.address: Devils Postpile National Monument, PO Box 3999, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Park grounds are open 24 hours daily during the season; Ranger Station typically 9:00 am – 5:00 pm
attractions.days_open: Daily during operating season (typically mid-June to mid-October)
attractions.admission: Shuttle Day Pass: $15/adult, $7/child (age 3–15); Private Vehicle (Exception/Off-Hours): $10/vehicle
highlights: 101-foot drop on the Middle Fork of the San Joaquin River
Frequent rainbows appearing in the waterfall's mist
Accessible via a moderate 2.5-mile hike from the ranger station
Part of the Devils Postpile National Monument
Access to Lower Falls via a downstream trail
Connection to the John Muir and Ansel Adams Wilderness areas
attractions.tips: Visit midday when the sun is highest for the best rainbow views. Bring plenty of water and sunscreen as the trail has little shade and can be very hot and dry. Most visitors must take the mandatory Reds Meadow Shuttle from the Mammoth Mountain Adventure Center
attractions.nearby: Devils Postpile, Reds Meadow Resort, Soda Springs Meadow, Minaret Falls
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open seasonally, typically from mid-June through mid-October; road and facilities close in winter due to snow

attractions.name: Rick's Sport Center
attractions.category: Shop (Sporting Goods, Fishing, and Ski/Snowboard Rentals)
attractions.description: Founded in 1991, Rick's Sport Center is a family-owned destination in Mammoth Lakes specializing in fishing and winter sports. The shop provides a comprehensive selection of trout-fishing gear, bait, and official licenses, alongside affordable ski and snowboard rental packages. With a focus on knowledgeable local service, they also offer guided fishing trips and expert tips to help visitors make the most of the Eastern Sierra outdoors
attractions.address: 3241 Main St, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Daily: 8:00 am – 6:00 pm
attractions.admission: Free (Store entrance); Rentals: Adult ski/snowboard from $29/day, Junior from $19/day, Fishing poles $10/day
highlights: Full-service trout-fishing bait and tackle center
Affordable daily ski and snowboard rental packages
Official California fishing licenses available on-site
Guided fly-fishing trips through Sierra Trout Outdoorsman
Expert local fishing reports and advice from experienced staff
Complimentary air compressor for float tube users
attractions.tips: Rent your winter gear here for some of the lowest prices in the area, often significantly cheaper than mountain-side shops. For anglers, check in for the latest 'fish report' and local hot spots before heading to the Lakes Basin. If you are using a float tube, take advantage of their free air fill-up service
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Lakes Basin, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, Devils Postpile National Monument, Rainbow Falls, and The Village at Mammoth
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; transitions from ski and snowboard rentals (December to mid-April) to fishing gear and supplies (late April to mid-November). Fishing season typically opens the last Saturday of April

attractions.name: Roberto's Cafe
attractions.description: Family owned and operated since 1985, Roberto's Cafe is a staple in Mammoth Lakes known for its authentic Mexican cuisine and vibrant atmosphere. The restaurant features a lower-level dining room, an artistic upstairs cantina with stunning Sierra views, and a seasonal garden patio surrounded by wildflowers. Its menu highlights include a wide variety of traditional dishes, unique seafood options like lobster and duck burritos, and an extensive selection of tequila and craft margaritas
attractions.address: 271 Old Mammoth Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Tuesday–Sunday: 11:00 AM – Close (typically 10:00 PM); Closed Mondays; Happy Hour: Daily 2:00 PM – 5:00 PM (except holidays)
attractions.admission: Free; price varies by menu item
highlights: Authentic Mexican favorites including Chile Verde, Carnitas, and Homemade Tamales
Signature "Huge" burritos with specialty fillings like savory duck and lobster
Artistic upstairs cantina offering epic views of the surrounding mountains
Seasonal garden patio seating, particularly scenic during wildflower season
Daily Happy Hour from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM with food and drink specials
Extensive drink menu featuring 'Killer Margaritas' and a large tequila selection
attractions.tips: The restaurant does not take reservations, so expect waits during peak times. The burritos are notably large and suitable for sharing if you haven't worked up a massive appetite. Note that Happy Hour specials are not available during holiday periods
attractions.nearby: Shea Schat's Bakery, Mono Arts Council, Mammoth Rock N Bowl, Minaret Cinemas
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; the garden patio is a highlight in the summer, while the upstairs cantina is a favorite for skiers and sports fans in the winter

attractions.name: Shady Rest Park
attractions.description: Shady Rest Park is a comprehensive community recreation area situated approximately one-half mile from Highway 203. It serves as a central hub for outdoor activities, offering diverse amenities from sports fields to specialized play areas. The park is highly valued by locals and visitors alike for its accessibility to the town's extensive trail system and its scenic forest setting
attractions.address: Sawmill Cutoff Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Dawn till dusk daily; Summer season typically runs from mid-May through November 1
attractions.admission: Free for personal use; fees apply for facility rentals
highlights: 18-hole pro-level DiscGolfPark
Street-scene skate park designed for all ability levels
Extensive sports facilities including soccer fields, softball/baseball diamonds, and sand volleyball courts
Large playground and sheltered picnic areas with BBQ grills
Groomed winter trails for cross-country skiing and snowshoeing
attractions.tips: The park is an excellent location for stargazing at night away from town lights, though visitors should be mindful of seasonal pollen levels in early summer. Parking is free and widely available
attractions.nearby: Shady Rest Campground, Mammoth Lakes Visitor Center and Ranger Station, and the various shops and restaurants in the town of Mammoth Lakes
attractions.seasonal_notes: Summer facilities are open May through November; winter trails are groomed 5 days a week when snow conditions permit

attractions.name: Shea Schat's Bakery
attractions.description: Shea Schat's Bakery is a beloved old-world style establishment in Mammoth Lakes, famous for its artisanal Dutch-inspired breads and extensive selection of sweet treats. It is a mandatory stop for many travelers in the Eastern Sierra, offering everything from hearty sandwiches to giant apple fritters and specialized loaves. The bakery maintains a family tradition of high-quality baking that has been a staple of the region for decades
attractions.address: 3305 Main Street, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM daily
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Famous Original Sheepherder Bread baked fresh daily
Hand-crafted sandwiches made with freshly sliced meats on house-baked bread
Extensive pastry selection including giant apple fritters and bear claws
Fresh-squeezed orange juice and a variety of gourmet coffees
Homemade candies, truffles, and specialty cookies
Outdoor dog-friendly seating area for visitors
attractions.tips: Arrive early in the morning to beat the long lines, especially during peak season, and to ensure the best selection of donuts and pastries. Every full sandwich typically comes with a complimentary cookie and pickle. Don't miss the 'day-old' rack for discounted loaves of their famous bread
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Brewing Company, The Village at Mammoth, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, Mammoth Creek Park
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; highly popular during both summer hiking and winter ski seasons

attractions.name: Shelter Distilling
attractions.description: Shelter Distilling is a craft distillery and cocktail restaurant located in the heart of The Village at Mammoth. It specializes in house-made spirits, including whiskey, gin, and agave spirits, paired with a handcrafted menu of pizzas, tacos, and burgers. The venue offers a welcoming distillery experience perfect for après-ski gatherings and live music
attractions.address: 100 Canyon Blvd. #217, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Sunday – Thursday: 11:00 am – 10:00 pm; Friday – Saturday: 11:00 am – 11:00 pm
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: House-distilled spirits including award-winning American Single Malt Whiskey and agave spirits
On-site working brewery with rotating house-made beers and ciders
Handcrafted food menu featuring smashburgers, pizzas, and globally-inspired tacos
Located in the bustling Village at Mammoth with an outdoor patio
Live music events and a vibrant après-ski atmosphere
Spirits available by the bottle for purchase and shipping within California
attractions.tips: Try the popular Pork Belly Tacos or the Smashburger with High Sierra Whiskey Burger Sauce. All cocktails are made with spirits distilled on-site, so it's a great place to sample their unique agave and whiskey flights
attractions.nearby: Village Gondola, Mammoth Mountain Ski Resort, Devils Creek Distillery
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round

attractions.name: Sherwin Lakes Trail
attractions.description: The Sherwin Lakes Trail is a moderately steep 3-mile climb (5.2 miles round-trip) leading to a pair of sub-alpine lakes on a glaciated bench. The trail offers sweeping vistas of the town of Mammoth Lakes and the Long Valley Caldera, winding through aspen groves and Jeffrey pines. It is a popular destination for hikers, anglers, and fall foliage enthusiasts
attractions.address: Sherwin Lakes Trailhead, Sherwin Creek Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Day use only; accessible 24/7 during open seasons
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Pair of sub-alpine lakes at 8,269 feet elevation
Sweeping vistas of the Long Valley Caldera and Mammoth Lakes town
Vibrant fall colors from surrounding aspen groves
Accessible spring/summer hiking at a relatively lower elevation
Connects to the John Muir Wilderness and Valentine Lake trail
Dog-friendly on-leash trail with free trailhead parking
attractions.tips: The trail is largely unshaded and can get very hot in mid-summer; bring plenty of water. Pack a lunch for a lakeside picnic. Wear sturdy hiking shoes for rocky switchbacks and sections with boulders. Water from lakes should be treated before drinking
attractions.nearby: Sherwin Creek Campground, Sherwin Meadows Trailhead, Valentine Lake Trail, Hayden Cabin Museum
attractions.seasonal_notes: Best visited in spring, summer, and fall; trail may be impassable or closed in winter due to snow. Aspens peak in mid-September to mid-October

attractions.name: Tamarack Cross-Country Ski Center
attractions.description: Tamarack Cross-Country Ski Center offers an immersive winter experience in the scenic Mammoth Lakes Basin with over 19 miles (32km) of world-class, groomed trails for Nordic skiing and snowshoeing. Located at the historic Tamarack Lodge, the center provides a full range of services including rentals, private lessons, and guided tours for all ability levels. Visitors can enjoy secluded tracks that meander through national forests, around frozen lakes, and beneath rugged cliffs
attractions.address: 163 Twin Lakes Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.days_open: Open daily during the winter season
attractions.admission: Prices vary based on regular, peak, and holiday periods. Adult Full Day: $39–$49; Adult Afternoon (after 1:00 PM): $29–$37. Young Adult (13-22) & Senior (65-79) Full Day: $31–$39; Afternoon: $23–$29. Child (5-12) Full Day/Afternoon: $10. Seniors 80+ and children 4 & under: Free,. 20% discounts for Ikon Pass holders and Mammoth Lodging Collection guests
highlights: 19+ miles (32km) of groomed tracks for cross-country skiing and snowshoeing
Guided Full Moon and Sunset snowshoe tours
Free US Forest Service volunteer naturalist-led tours
Professional ski school offering private lessons and multi-week clinics
Convenient on-site rentals and gear shop at the Tamarack Yurt
Direct access to the scenic Twin Lakes and Mammoth Lakes Basin
attractions.tips: Take the free ESTA Orange Line shuttle from The Village to reach the lodge and avoid limited parking. Purchase trail passes online in advance for the best rates, as same-day tickets at the yurt may be higher. Afternoon passes (after 1:00 PM) offer a discounted way to enjoy the trails with fewer crowds. Sun protection (SPF and sunglasses) is essential due to high-altitude snow glare
attractions.nearby: Tamarack Lodge, The Lakefront Restaurant, Twin Lakes, Lake Mary, Horseshoe Lake, and Mammoth Mountain Ski Area
attractions.seasonal_notes: Winter only; typically scheduled to operate from late December through mid-April, depending on snow conditions

attractions.name: The Booky Joint
attractions.description: The Booky Joint is an independent, locally owned bookstore and toy shop that has been serving the community since 1978. It offers a wide selection of fiction and nonfiction, children's books, art supplies, puzzles, and a large collection of Legos. Located in the Minaret Village Mall, it provides a cozy setting for finding local interest books, maps, and gifts
attractions.address: 437 Old Mammoth Rd, Suite F, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 10:00 AM - 06:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Independent and locally owned bookstore since 1978
Extensive collection of Legos and children's toys
Wide variety of new and used books across all genres
Art supplies, puzzles, games, and stationery
Selection of local travel, hiking maps, and regional interest books
Located conveniently in Minaret Village Mall next to the movie theaters
attractions.tips: It's a great place to pick up local hiking maps and regional books before heading out on trails. The store is dog-friendly and known for its friendly, knowledgeable staff. Check out the used book section for great deals on classics and local titles
attractions.nearby: Minaret Village Mall movie theaters, Giovanni's Italian Restaurant, Mammoth Rock N Bowl, Vons
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round

attractions.name: The Breakfast Club
attractions.description: The Breakfast Club is a longtime Mammoth Lakes institution known for its classic American breakfast favorites and hearty lunch options served with a fresh, modern twist. Under new ownership, the restaurant maintains a cozy, 'home away from home' atmosphere with unique cow-themed decor and a reputation for massive portions. It is a popular pre-ski or pre-hike stop for both locals and visitors looking for high-quality ingredients and friendly service
attractions.address: 2987 Main St, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546-6169
attractions.hours: 7:00 AM - 2:00 PM (Daily)
attractions.admission: Free (Prices vary by menu item, typically $12 - $20)
highlights: Famous biscuits and spicy gravy
Hearty omelets and breakfast burritos
Fresh-baked pastries including bear claws and cinnamon rolls
Full espresso bar with lattes and cappuccinos
Unique cow-themed 'elderly auntie's dining room' decor
Large portions and friendly, family-like service
attractions.tips: The restaurant was traditionally cash-only but now accepts credit cards and digital payments. It can get very busy on weekends and powder days, so arrive early to avoid long waits. Try the cinnamon roll or a croissant instead of standard toast for a special treat
attractions.nearby: Outbound Mammoth, Shilo Inns, Mogul Restaurant, Good Life Cafe, Shea Schat's Bakery
attractions.seasonal_notes: Year-round

attractions.name: The Gallery at Twin Lakes
attractions.description: Housed in the historic 1934 cabin of photographer and painter Stephen H. Willard, this world-class gallery showcases fine art, antiques, and mining memorabilia. The collection focuses on historic Eastern Sierra and Yosemite paintings by museum-level California artists such as Edgar Payne and Maynard Dixon. It offers a unique cultural experience nestled within the scenic beauty of the Mammoth Lakes Basin
attractions.address: 2749 Lake Mary Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Located in the historic 1934 cabin of Stephen H. Willard
Focus on Eastern Sierra and Yosemite landscape paintings
Collection of museum-level historic California painters like Edgar Payne and Maynard Dixon
Exhibits of local antiques and historic mining memorabilia
Unique alpine setting in the heart of the Mammoth Lakes Basin
attractions.tips: Check for seasonal opening dates before visiting, as the gallery is closed during the winter and spring months. It is an excellent stop for art lovers and history buffs exploring the Lakes Basin
attractions.nearby: Twin Lakes Campground, Twin Falls Overlook, Tamarack Lodge, Lake Mary, and the Dragon's Back Trail
attractions.seasonal_notes: Closed during the Winter and Spring seasons; typically open during the Summer and Fall when the Lakes Basin roads are accessible

attractions.name: The Lakefront Restaurant at Tamarack Lodge
attractions.description: The Lakefront Restaurant is an intimate fine dining destination perched on the banks of Twin Lakes inside the historic Tamarack Lodge. It offers a fresh, seasonal menu with French-California influences, an extensive wine collection, and extraordinary service in a rustic mountain setting. The restaurant is renowned as one of the most romantic and secluded spots in the Eastern Sierra
attractions.address: 163 Twin Lakes Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Wednesday–Sunday: Lunch 12:00 PM–3:00 PM, Dinner 4:30 PM–8:00 PM; Monday–Tuesday: Closed
attractions.admission: Price varies (fine dining menu); lunch items approx. $20-30, dinner entrees $40-$60+; corkage fee applies
highlights: Stunning forested views overlooking the banks of Twin Lakes
Intimate dining room with a handful of tables inside the historic 1920s Tamarack Lodge
French-California cuisine using locally-grown, seasonal produce
Live music and classic tunes in the lodge lobby from Wednesday to Saturday, 6:00 PM–8:00 PM
Awarded \"Best Dinner in the Eastern Sierra\" by Sunset Magazine
Boutique lounge food options and full bar available for lobby service during dinner hours
attractions.tips: Dinner reservations are highly recommended and can be made by calling 760.914.7157. Arrive early to enjoy the lobby fireplace or catch the views during daylight hours. Note that Mammoth Mountain is a cashless resort, accepting major credit cards and digital payments only
attractions.nearby: Tamarack Cross Country Ski Center, Twin Lakes Trails, Lake Mary, Horseshoe Lake, Panorama Dome
attractions.seasonal_notes: The restaurant operates year-round with seasonal menu rotations; lunch and outdoor seating are available weather-permitting

attractions.name: The Mogul Restaurant
attractions.description: The Mogul Restaurant is a historic Mammoth Lakes steakhouse that has been serving the community since 1971. Known for its cozy, rustic mountain-cabin atmosphere, it specializes in USDA Choice steaks, slow-roasted prime rib, and fresh seafood. A unique feature of the restaurant is that the waiters often grill the entrees to order, providing a personalized and high-quality dining experience ()
attractions.address: 1528 Tavern Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Bar: 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Dinner: 5:30 PM – 9:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free entry; menu prices vary (Entrees typically range from $29.95 to $77.95+)
highlights: Signature slow-roasted Prime Rib served nightly until sold out
Unique 'waiter-grilled' steaks and entrees prepared to your exact preference
Fresh, well-appointed salad bar featuring a wide variety of ingredients
The 'Cinnamon Charlotte,' a house specialty dessert since 1971
Full bar with a selection of premium California wines and classic cocktails
Family-friendly environment with a dedicated children's menu
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially during peak ski season and weekends. Guests paying with cash receive a 3.38% discount on their bill. Don't miss the Cinnamon Charlotte dessert, a local favorite for over 50 years (,)
attractions.nearby: Nik-n-Willies Pizza, Outbound Mammoth, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, The Village at Mammoth
attractions.seasonal_notes: Generally open year-round, though the restaurant may observe seasonal closures or reduced days (such as Tuesdays) during the shoulder seasons in May or October

attractions.name: The Restaurant at Convict Lake
attractions.description: The Restaurant at Convict Lake is widely recognized as one of the finest dining establishments in the Eastern Sierra, offering a romantic and casually elegant atmosphere. The menu features continental cuisine with diverse selections such as Beef Wellington, rack of lamb, and fresh rainbow trout. Located just 10 minutes from Mammoth Lakes, it provides a sophisticated dining experience nestled in the scenic Convict Canyon
attractions.address: 2000 Convict Lake Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Lounge: Daily from 5:00 PM; Dining Room Seating: Starts at 5:30 PM; Last Seating: Mon-Thu at 8:00 PM, Fri-Sun at 8:30 PM
attractions.admission: Price varies (Fine dining; entrees typically range from $30 to $60+)
highlights: Signature Beef Wellington and New Zealand Rack of Lamb
Award-winning wine list and specialty martini bar
Tableside flambéed Bananas Foster dessert
Casually elegant mountain ambiance with a cozy fireplace
Gourmet pizza menu available seasonally in the lounge
Waterfront proximity to the scenic Convict Lake
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended as the restaurant is a popular choice for both locals and visitors. Arrive early to enjoy a walk around Convict Lake before dinner, and drive carefully as deer are frequently seen on the road leading to the resort
attractions.nearby: Convict Lake, Convict Lake Campground, Hot Creek, Hot Creek Fish Hatchery, Mammoth Lakes
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round with a seasonal closure typically from late November to mid-December (e.g., Nov 30 – Dec 17). Gourmet Pizza is served from the end of April through October

attractions.name: The Sierra Light Gallery
attractions.description: The Sierra Light Gallery features the award-winning landscape photography of Nolan Nitschke, focusing on the Eastern Sierra, Yosemite, and the American West. The gallery showcases large-format fine art prints, including limited edition acrylic and metal pieces, in a modern mountain setting. Visitors can explore a vast collection of local scenes captured in stunning detail and vibrant color
attractions.address: 3343 Main St. Ste. C1, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Winter Hours: Monday 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday CLOSED, Wednesday-Saturday 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM. Hours vary seasonally and may be affected by winter storms
attractions.days_open: Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Award-winning landscape photography by local artist Nolan Nitschke
Large-format fine art prints available in sizes up to 100 inches
Extensive collections of the Eastern Sierra, Yosemite National Park, and Mt. Whitney
Specialized print materials including master anti-reflection acrylic and metal
Custom consultations for interior design and specialized sizing
attractions.tips: Check the official website for the most current seasonal hours before visiting. If you are looking for a specific size for your home, take advantage of the gallery's interior design consultation services. The gallery is located next to Black Velvet Coffee, making it a great stop for art and a drink
attractions.nearby: Black Velvet Coffee, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, Mammoth Lakes Basin, Devils Postpile National Monument, Hot Creek Geologic Site
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; hours are seasonal and subject to change based on weather conditions and winter storms

attractions.name: The Stove Restaurant
attractions.description: The Stove Restaurant is a legendary Mammoth Lakes breakfast and lunch spot that has been serving hearty, homestyle American food for over 50 years. The diner features a charming, country-themed interior with farmhouse decor, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere for both locals and visitors
attractions.address: 644 Old Mammoth Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: 7:00 am - 2:00 pm daily
attractions.admission: Free; menu prices vary typically between $8 and $20
highlights: Known for signature skillet potatoes, which are hand-peeled, boiled, and sliced in-house daily
Famous for "cutie pies" (miniature three-inch pies) and a variety of fresh, whole-baked pies
Signature breakfast items include cinnamon swirl French toast, Sierra Sunrise skillets, and fluffy biscuits with homemade gravy
A long-standing Mammoth tradition since the early 1970s, maintaining a consistent menu and local vibe
Extremely family-friendly with large booths, highchairs, and a dedicated kids' menu
attractions.tips: Expect a wait on weekends and during peak hours as no reservations are accepted; take-out is not available when the restaurant is on a wait,. Be sure to try their freshly made preserves and jams with your biscuits
attractions.nearby: Mammoth Rock N Bowl, Mammoth Creek Park, and Mono Arts Council
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; specifically noted as open 7 days a week during summer and winter seasons

attractions.name: The Village at Mammoth
attractions.description: The Village at Mammoth is a premier year-round event destination and social hub in the heart of Mammoth Lakes, California. It features a diverse collection of over 14 eateries and 10 retailers, along with luxury condominium lodging at The Village Lodge and The Westin Monache Resort. The village also serves as a central transportation hub, providing direct gondola access to the ski slopes in winter and hosting numerous festivals and activities in summer
attractions.address: 6201 Minaret Road, Mammoth Lakes, California 93546
attractions.hours: Individual shop and restaurant hours vary; most open daily from 10:00 AM or 11:00 AM until late evening. The Village Lodge front desk is open 24/7
attractions.admission: Free entry; individual business prices vary. The Village Gondola is free for non-skiers
highlights: Over 14 diverse eateries ranging from casual to fine dining
10 unique retail shops featuring apparel, art, and mountain gear
Direct Village Gondola access to Canyon Lodge during the winter season
Year-round event destination hosting festivals, live music, and outdoor movies
Luxury lodging options at The Village Lodge and Westin Monache Resort
Central hub for the free town shuttle system
attractions.tips: Utilize the free town shuttle (Stop #90 or #18) to avoid parking fees. Complimentary underground parking is available for guests staying at The Village Lodge
attractions.nearby: Canyon Lodge, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, Mammoth Bike Park, Mammoth Yosemite Airport
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; summer features festivals and hiking/biking access, while winter offers ski-in access and holiday events

attractions.name: The Warming Hut
attractions.description: The Warming Hut is a family owned and operated restaurant in Mammoth Lakes serving craft comfort food made almost exclusively from scratch. The restaurant's name is a tribute to Dave and Roma McCoy’s original lodge at Mammoth Mountain, offering a "Modern Mountain" vibe with a cozy fireplace in the winter and a sunny outdoor deck in the summer
attractions.address: 343 Old Mammoth Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Breakfast: 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Lunch: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Dinner: 5:00 PM - 8:00 PM; Happy Hour/Apres Ski (Winter only): 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM
attractions.admission: Price varies; breakfast items range from $8 to $20, lunch from $10 to $18, and dinner entrées from $15 to $40
highlights: Craft comfort food made from scratch using fresh ingredients
Authentic Vermont maple syrup and house-made sugar-free ketchup
"Do It Yourself" breakfast section for custom meals
Cozy interior with a fireplace and an expansive outdoor patio
Full bar featuring signature cocktails and local beer on tap
Pet-friendly outdoor seating area
attractions.tips: Try the custom "Warming Hut Blend" coffee from local Looney Bean, and don't miss the house-made Reuben or the garlic parmesan fries. On weekends, arrive early (around 9:00 AM) to avoid long wait times at this popular local spot
attractions.nearby: Mono Arts Council, Mammoth Rock N Bowl, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, and the Lakes Basin (Lake Mary, Duck Pass)
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; Happy Hour and Apres Ski menus are typically offered during the winter season only

attractions.name: Toomey's Mammoth Lakes
attractions.description: Toomey's is an iconic, flavor-forward restaurant in Mammoth Lakes where bold, inventive cooking meets a laid-back mountain energy. Founded by Chef Matt Toomey, a local culinary legend, the eatery offers a unique menu that blends California freshness with Hawaiian comfort and classic American hearty fare. Since opening in 2012, it has become a town staple, known for its friendly staff, casual atmosphere, and signature dishes like coconut mascarpone pancakes and seafood jambalaya
attractions.address: 6085 Minaret Rd, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Daily: 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM (Breakfast typically served until 11:00 AM; Lunch & Dinner 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM)
attractions.admission: Price varies (Menu items typically $15 - $40)
highlights: Signature Coconut Mascarpone Pancakes and Smoked Trout Bagels for breakfast
Famous Seafood Jambalaya, Buffalo Meatloaf, and Lobster Taquitos
Eclectic menu featuring fresh fish, wild game, and unique mountain-inspired creations
Baseball-themed decor with a lively, casual, and family-friendly atmosphere
Outdoor seating available, including dog-friendly patio areas
attractions.tips: No reservations are accepted due to limited seating, so arrive early during peak season; outdoor seating is dog-friendly; try the lobster taquitos or coconut pancakes, which are local favorites
attractions.nearby: The Village at Mammoth, Mammoth Mountain Ski Area, Lake Mary, Woolly's Tube Park, Clocktower Cellar
attractions.seasonal_notes: Open year-round

attractions.name: Twin Lakes
attractions.description: Twin Lakes is a scenic recreation area featuring two alpine lakes connected by the spectacular 287-foot Twin Falls at the base of Mammoth Mountain. It is a popular destination for trophy trout fishing, boating, and camping within a lush pine forest. The area offers accessible trails, charming footbridges, and panoramic mountain views, making it a favorite for families and nature photographers
attractions.address: Twin Lakes Road & Lake Mary Road, Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours for lake and trail access; Campground check-in/out times apply (Checkout by noon); Store hours vary
attractions.admission: Free for lake access and trails; $34/night for single campsite, $10/night for additional vehicle
